title: 17 Settlement and Adjustments of Deposits
body_text:
17	Settlement and Adjustments of Deposits
17.1	Settlement and Reconciliation of Deposits to Your Financial Institution芒聙聶s Central聽1 Account
Canadian and US dollar deposits are credited to your financial institution芒聙聶s account on the day that the deposits are received and processed at Central 1 during the following dates and times:
Branch Capture and Corporate Capture image exchange are accepted at Central 1 from Monday to Friday, excluding Canadian statutory holidays.
Branch Capture and Corporate Capture can be made any time but are only processed on business days. Branch Capture and Corporate Capture deposits made after 7 pm PT are processed on the next business day.
US dollar deposits drawn on US financial institutions aren芒聙聶t processed on US statutory holidays.
Mobile deposits can be made at any time but are only processed on business days. Mobile deposits made after 6 pm PT are processed on the following business day.
Deposits are credited to your financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account as follows:
For deposits submitted via Branch Capture, a virtual deposit slip is automatically created in Branch Capture.
For deposits submitted via Corporate Capture, a virtual deposit slip is automatically created on the Corporate Capture application.
For mobile deposits, Central 1 creates electronic deposit slips that include up to 200 deposits.
Regardless of which branch the depositing customer is associated with, all mobile deposits are credited to a single transit number as specified by your financial institution.
Corporate Capture deposits are credited to the transit number your financial institution designates for the business customer芒聙聶s Corporate Capture deposit settlement.
Branch Capture deposits appear on your financial institution芒聙聶s statement (AIBC/AIBU 芒聙聯 BC, AIOC/AIOU 芒聙聯 Ontario) as code 芒聙聹BC芒聙聺.
Mobile deposits appear on your financial institution芒聙聶s statement (AIBC/AIBU 芒聙聯 BC, AIOC/AIOU 芒聙聯 Ontario) as code 芒聙聹RM芒聙聺.
Corporate Capture deposits appear on your financial institution芒聙聶s statement (AIBC/AIBU 芒聙聯 BC, AIOC/AIOU 芒聙聯 Ontario) as code 芒聙聹RC芒聙聺.
Canadian dollar deposits are credited to your financial institution芒聙聶s Canadian dollar account.
US dollar deposits are credited to your financial institution芒聙聶s US dollar account.
Settlement Delays for Items Processed via Branch Capture and Corporate Capture
Settlement to your financial institution芒聙聶s account can be delayed if
deposits aren芒聙聶t balanced (e.g., are forced balanced),
deposits are transmitted to Central 1 after the 7:00 pm local time deadline,
cheques are transmitted to Central 1 in the incorrect deposit type (e.g., Canadian item included in a US dollar deposit),
item isn芒聙聶t eligible for clearing,
item amount was keyed incorrectly,
a piggyback was included in the deposit, or
images of cheques are poor quality.
Settlement Delays for Items Processed via Mobile Deposits
Settlement to your financial institution芒聙聶s account can be delayed only if images of cheques are poor quality.
Canadian Dollar Deposit Reports
Use the ADC 芒聙聯 Acknowledgement of Canadian Dollar Deposits report to reconcile Canadian dollar deposits to your financial institution芒聙聶s Canadian dollar account at Central 1. This applies to mobile deposits and paper deposits submitted via Branch Capture or Corporate Capture.
For Canadian dollar deposits processed via Branch Capture and Corporate Capture, see the RDBC 芒聙聯 Remote Deposit System Branch Capture Deposits report. The RDBC report lists Canadian dollar deposit details and summary information for items captured and transmitted to Central 1 before 7 pm local time Monday to Friday, excluding statutory holidays. See Section 18.2, ADC 芒聙聯 Acknowledgement of Canadian Dollar Deposits, and Section 18.5, RDBC 芒聙聯 Remote Deposit System Branch Capture Deposits, for more information.
For Canadian dollar Mobile Deposits, also refer to the RDLC 芒聙聯 Remote Deposit System, Mobile Remote Deposits report. The RDCL lists individual Mobile Deposit transactions. See Section 18.4, RDLC 芒聙聯 Remote Deposit System, Mobile Remote Deposits.
US Dollar Deposit Reports
Use the ADUS 芒聙聯 Acknowledgement of US Dollar Deposits to reconcile US dollar deposits to your financial institution芒聙聶s US dollar account at Central 1. This applies to paper deposits submitted via Branch Capture or Corporate Capture.
For US dollar deposits processed via Branch Capture and/or Corporate Capture, see the RDBU 芒聙聯 Remote Deposit System Branch Capture Deposits report. The RDBU report lists the US dollar deposit details and summary information items captured and transmitted to Central 1 before 7 pm local time Monday to Friday, excluding statutory holidays. See Section 18.3, ADUS 芒聙聯 Acknowledgement of US Dollar Deposits, and Section 18.6, RDBU 芒聙聯 Remote Deposit System Branch Capture Deposits, for more information.
Note:	Deposit adjustments are included in the acknowledgement reports. They appear as 芒聙聹Debit Adjustment芒聙聺 or 芒聙聹Credit Adjustment.芒聙聺
17.2	Settlement of Deposits to Customers芒聙聶 Accounts
Your financial institution is responsible for settlement to your customer芒聙聶s accounts.
Branch Capture
Credit the customer芒聙聶s account for each deposit item. Hold funds as per your internal policy.
For US dollar deposits, credit the customer芒聙聶s US dollar account or convert the funds to Canadian dollars and credit their Canadian dollar account. Determine the exchange rate if settling with the customer in Canadian dollars.
Corporate Capture
Corporate Capture deposits made after 7 pm local time are processed on the following business day.
If your financial institution participates in real-time messaging, you can use the real-time message to post to the customer芒聙聶s account.
If your financial institution doesn芒聙聶t participate in real-time message processing, you have the option of using the Corporate Capture Deposit File to post to the host banking system. Alternatively, you can use the Corporate Capture Deposit File to manually credit customers芒聙聶 accounts.
Regardless of whether you use real-time messaging, the Corporate Capture Deposit File should be used for settlement with the customer (as described above). It芒聙聶s the only exact list of deposits and may include deposits that were dropped with the real-time messaging service. The amount in the Corporate Capture Deposit File is the declared amount as entered by the business customer when creating the deposit. This allows you to correlate deposits to the real-time messages sent with the deposit. If the amount is incorrect, Central 1 will create an adjustment which displays in the Online Return System (ORS).
Central 1 recommends that your financial institution hold funds on Corporate Capture deposits like it does on ATM items. The exact length of the hold is the responsibility of your financial institution.
Mobile Deposits
Mobile deposits made after 6 pm PT are processed on the following business day.
If your financial institution participates in real-time messaging, you can opt to have settlement to the customer芒聙聶s account posted immediately.
If your financial institution doesn芒聙聶t participate in real-time messaging, the customer芒聙聶s accounts are automatically credited when your financial institution imports the Mobile Deposit File to the host banking system. Alternatively, use the RDLC Report to manually credit customers芒聙聶 accounts. See Section 14.6, Mobile Deposit File, and Section 18.4, RDLC 芒聙聯 Remote Deposit System, Mobile Remote Deposits.
Central 1 recommends that your financial institution hold funds on mobile deposits like it does on ATM items. The exact length of the hold is the responsibility of your financial institution.
17.3	Deposit Adjustments
Adjustments aren芒聙聶t made for $1 or less. Central 1 adjusts the amount of a deposit if it identifies one of the following errors.
For Mobile Deposits
A customer entered an incorrect amount on the mobile banking application when making a mobile deposit. Use the adjustment slip provided by Central 1 to correct the amount credited to the customer芒聙聶s account.
For Items Deposited to Central 1 via Branch Capture and Corporate Capture
An item was deposited that can芒聙聶t be processed (e.g., image quality problems, piggybacks).
An item amount was keyed incorrectly, and a deposit was force balanced.
An item was received that shouldn芒聙聶t have been included in the deposit (e.g., the currency of the item doesn芒聙聶t match the currency of the deposit type, the item isn芒聙聶t eligible for clearing).
Adjustment Advices
You can view an adjustment advice on the Online Return System (ORS).
If an adjustment is created for items that shouldn芒聙聶t have been included in the deposit, the item can also be viewed on ORS. The item will have a value of $0.00 and a cheque transit of 芒聙聹11111111.芒聙聺 The reason for the adjustment is captured on the adjustment advice. Other types of supporting documents aren芒聙聶t displayed on ORS. See Figure 1. See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures on using ORS.
If the adjustment was the result of an item that should have been included in the deposit, the item will need to be redeposited. The original item must be retrieved, recaptured, and transmitted for processing in Branch Capture or Corporate Capture.
Note: 	For Corporate Capture adjustments, your financial institution will adjust the business customer芒聙聶s account and notify the business customer of the adjustments. Your financial institution will instruct your business customer to retrieve the original item and redeposit the item(s) if necessary.
Figure 1: Deposit Adjustment
Deposit adjustments are included in the acknowledgement reports (ADC or ADUS). They appear as 芒聙聹Debit Adjustment芒聙聺 or 芒聙聹Credit Adjustment芒聙聺. Central 1 posts the total amount of each adjustment to your financial institution芒聙聶s account separately.
Note:	Deposit adjustments are subject to fees. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725. All other clients, contact your relationship manager.
END
